channel.title: La Señal — Español por input comprensible
channel.description: A serialized sci-fi political drama in Colombian and Venezuelan Spanish, engineered as a 365-day comprehensible-input curriculum. A Bogotá astronomer detects a signal that is not from Earth — and the fight over what it means begins. Starts slow and scaffolded, ends at full native speed. Voice references: OpenSLR SLR72/SLR75 (CC-BY-SA 4.0, © Google). Private listening feed.
channel.itunes:author: La Señal

item.description: The dish is fixed, the coffee is hot, and three people wait in the dark for 3:47 AM. What happens next is five minutes late — and it changes everything.

item.title: Manos a la obra
item.description: Two days of repairs, one story told in pieces: Caracas, the blackouts, and the long road to a hill outside Bogotá. Then Andrés asks the one question nobody else thought to ask.

item.title: El venezolano
item.description: The repair company sends an engineer to fix the storm damage. He arrives with a toolbox, a thermos he guards with his life, and opinions about arepas. Nothing at the observatory will be the same.

item.title: La tormenta
item.description: A violent storm hits the hills above Bogotá, and the observatory pays the price. If the signal returns at 3:47, will anyone be listening?

item.title: Día 5 — «El jefe»
item.description: Camila takes the pattern to her director and gets six words she will never forget: we need funds, not ghosts. On the ride home, for once, she is the one asking the taxi driver questions. Plus: who actually pays for astronomy.

item.title: Día 4 — «Otra vez»
item.description: Night shift at the observatory. Camila watches the clock crawl toward 3:47 — and the sky keeps its appointment. Two occurrences is not chance. Plus: coincidence versus pattern, or how scientists decide what is real.

item.title: Día 3 — «El taxi»
item.description: Meet El Flaco: taxi driver, conspiracy podcaster, and the fastest mouth in Bogotá. Camila half-listens to his wild theories — until one sentence about the sky makes her stop smiling. Plus: why Bogotá is cold on the equator.

item.title: Día 2 — «La rutina»
item.description: Camila's upside-down life: sleep by day, empanadas at doña Marta's, night shift at the dish. She replays the recording and sees something new — and her colleague Julio has one word for it. Plus: why astronomers work at night.

item.title: Día 1 — «El ruido»
item.description: Astronomer Camila Rey works alone at night in a small observatory above Bogotá — until, at 3:47 in the morning, her computers record a noise that should not exist. Plus: what a radio telescope actually does (hint: it hears nothing).

item.title: Episodio 0 — Prueba de nivel
item.description: A 12-minute listening diagnostic. Five passages, each faster and denser than the last, each followed by three spoken questions. Score yourself with the sheet in the show notes; your result sets Day 1. Instructions in English; everything else in Spanish.
